text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a software development practice where developers regularly merge their code changes into a shared repository. Every time new code is added, an automated build process is triggered. This involves compiling the code and running a series of tests to check for errors. The main goal is to catch issues early, before they become bigger problems in the project. These steps are organised into CI pipelines, which are sets of automated tasks that run in sequence. By using CI, teams can discover bugs quickly, improve code quality, and work together more efficiently.
